Berichten zufolge wurde China beim Reverse Engineering der DUV-Lithografie von ASML erwischt China reportedly caught reverse-engineering ASML’s DUV lithography Von …

Dutch technology journalist Mark Heikkin interviewed ASML leadership, who noted that as early as 2018, they had noticed Shanghai Microelectronics Corporation in China was working on immersion technology.
The equipment has the ability to manufacture 14nm and 7nm process chips and is technically similar to the immersion DUV lithography machine of Dutch semiconductor equipment giant ASML.
ASML also recently announced a strategic partnership with European AI startup Mistral AI to develop high-precision control software to enhance the performance of its lithography machines.

ASML announced its third-quarter 2025 results last week, with net sales reaching 7.5 billion euros, a gross profit margin of 51.6%, and a net profit of 2.1 billion euros, which was in line with guidance.
the Netherlands has also banned ASML from exporting its most advanced extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines to China.

which uses "immersion" technology similar to ASML. The report quoted sources as saying that the main components of the lithography machine have been locally produced, but some parts still need to be imported. However, it is believed that this can help China break through US export controls.
After ASML inspected the factory, they discovered that the lithography machine wasn't simply malfunctioning, but had been disassembled and reassembled.
attempting to study the structure and then mass-produce it themselves, but the reassembly failed, causing damage to the machine, so China turned to ASML for help.

After China reportedly dismantled its lithography machine and was unable to restore it, ASML requested help and sent personnel to China for repairs. The picture shows the ASML booth at the 2023 CIIE Expo in Shanghai. (Photo by Costfoto/NurPhoto via Getty Images) · NurPhoto via Getty Images

hoping to understand the structure of the machines and then produce them on their own. Unexpectedly, they were unable to restore the machines after dismantling them, and ultimately had to ask ASML for help with repairs.

Pero es que eso no es todo, realmente el Anticheat y el cheat ahora mismo están en una carrera armamentistica porque ambos tienen el mismo nivel de acceso al sistema. Entonces llega una cuarta generación. KLAC que exige TPM 2.0, Secureboot y un kernel firmado por Microsoft.

Y entonces llegamos a una tercera generación, los KLAC (kernel level AntiCheats). AntiCheats que se ejecutan en el kernel y pueden acceder a la memoria del mismo.

Estos serían EAC y Battleye.

Y este es el punto exacto en el que perdemos la compatibilidad con Linux (específico más adelante)

This increased exposure effect is highly dependent on particle sizes. Ventilation is always beneficial for the smallest aerosols and has no effect on very large. So the Pi 5 and 500 can't run RISC OS that runs on the previous models not because Broadcom changed anything in the GPU but because of the more fundamental limitation that the newer Cortex-A76 CPU core can only run unprivileged (user mode) code in 32-bit mode. All privileged code has to be 64-bit.

The interesting thing about the Raspberry Pi series is all of the CPUs use this Broadcom design with a programmable GPU called VideoCore, which runs proprietary firmware blobs and manages the OS boot and various functions besides graphics. So the OS makes VideoCore calls to run code on the GPU.
